concerns.text: FR-004 applies a Bonferroni correction for 30 hypothesis tests, implicitly assuming independence among tests. The composition variables (proton density, temperature, helium abundance) are physically correlated, and lagged correlations share autocorrelated time‑series structure, violating the independence assumption. This can make the correction overly conservative and distort false‑positive/negative rates. Recommend using a multiple‑testing method that accounts for dependence (e.g., Holm‑Bonferroni, Benjamini‑Hochberg, or a permutation‑based family‑wise error control).

SC-003 validates results on a held‑out 3‑year period by checking whether any composition‑index pair exceeds |r| > 0.5 and has a Bonferroni‑corrected p < 0.05, but it does not specify how the multiple‑testing correction is applied to the validation set. Re‑using the correction derived from the training set without recomputing for the validation set (or without accounting for the same dependence issues noted in FR‑004) can lead to mis‑estimated significance. Clarify the correction procedure for the validation period and ensure it respects the dependence structure of the lagged tests.
